sections.title: National Framework and Regulatory Decentralization
sections.contentHtml:
The foundational structure of the U.S. MLS system has shifted from a rigid top-down mandate to a model of "unfettered local discretion" following a comprehensive risk assessment conducted in late 2025. Following recommendations from the MLS Policy Risk Assessment Presidential Advisory Group (PAG) in November 2025, eighteen major policy repeals and updates were approved to mitigate antitrust exposure and modernize operations for the 2026 calendar year.

MLS Count — Verified Figure

As of early 2026, RESO tracks just over 500 MLS systems in the United States. The National Association of REALTORS® (NAR) oversees approximately 500 REALTOR-affiliated MLSs. Any reference to "580" in prior versions of this document was incorrect and has been removed. Use 'just over 500' in all JSON metadata fields.

The 18 NAR MLS Policy Changes — Full Reference

On November 14–16, 2025, during NAR NXT in Houston, NAR's Executive Committee approved 18 comprehensive updates to the MLS Policy Handbook — the most extensive update in 20 years. These changes took effect January 2026. Five policy changes with direct platform impact were missing from the prior version of this document and are added here.

CRITICAL ADDITION — POLICY 7.87 REPEAL: NAR repealed Policy Statement 7.87 (Transmittal of Participants' Listings to Aggregators and Public Sites). There is NO longer any national framework governing whether an MLS must transmit listing data to third-party portals or aggregators. Syndication is now entirely a local MLS decision. Platforms cannot assume any national syndication right — every market now requires a per-MLS syndication agreement.

PolicyChange
Platform ImpactPS 7.7 — Association Membership as MLS Prerequisite
REPEALEDMembership may still be required locally. Platforms must verify participation rules per MLS — no national standard.
PS 7.58 — IDX Display Limited to REALTORS® OnlyAMENDED — local restriction removed
MLSs can no longer limit IDX display rights exclusively to REALTOR® members. Broader IDX eligibility is now the default.PS 7.87 — Syndication to Third-Party Aggregators
REPEALED — CRITICAL FOR PLATFORMSNo national syndication framework exists. Every MLS sets its own syndication rules. Platforms must execute individual agreements per MLS.
PS 7.62 — Open Listings Excluded from MLSREPEALED
MLSs may now choose to accept open listings where permitted by state law. Update listing status logic accordingly.PS 7.89 — MLS Financial Penalty Cap ($15,000)
REPEALEDThe national $15,000 MLS violation fine cap is removed. Local MLSs now set their own fine schedules with no ceiling.
Section 5 — MLS Disciplinary GuidelinesFULLY REPEALED
Entire disciplinary framework removed from national handbook. Each MLS now governs its own discipline, fines, and enforcement.PS 7.71 & 7.73 — Offer Presentation Rules
REPEALED from MLS rulesOffer obligations now governed solely by NAR Code of Ethics and state law — not enforceable as MLS violations.

NAR Self-Certification Deadline — New in 2026

Unless indicated otherwise in the NAR 2026 MLS Handbook, all local MLS organizations were required to self-certify adoption of the updated Handbook policies by March 1, 2026. This deadline is not present in prior versions of this document and is critical for compliance auditing: any MLS that has not self-certified by this date is operating out of policy and presents elevated feed risk for marketplace platforms.

Participation Models — Thompson Broker Rule (FL, GA, AL)

Since 1991, Florida, Georgia, and Alabama have permitted non-REALTOR member access to the MLS via what is commonly called the "Thompson Broker" rule. In these three states, licensed brokers who are not NAR members retain the right to access MLS data and IDX feeds. Marketplace platforms operating in these states must account for Thompson Broker participants in their feed access verification logic.

StateParticipation Rule
NAR Membership Required?Compliance Note
FloridaThompson Broker (1991)
NoLicensed brokers get MLS access regardless of NAR membership.
GeorgiaThompson Broker (1991)
NoSame as Florida; verify broker license only.
AlabamaThompson Broker (1991)
NoSame as Florida; verify broker license only.
All Other StatesStandard NAR / Local MLS Rules
Varies by MLSCheck local MLS participation policy.

Access Mechanisms

Marketplace platforms interact with listing data through three primary mechanisms, each fundamentally altered by the August 17, 2024, antitrust settlement and subsequent 2026 policy updates.

Mechanism2026 Compliance Focus
Mandatory GovernanceIDX
Public display; strict compensation data filtering.NAR Policy 7.58; Local MLS IDX Rules.
VOWGated consumer access; registration and terms of use.
NAR VOW Policy; State Privacy Law.Syndication
Broad third-party distribution; seller opt-out priority.Participant Agreements; NAR MLOS Policy.
BBO FeedInternal analytics and back-office operations.
MLS Policy Statement 8.7.
